mapping.a_element: the cosmological constant / dark energy invoked as the origin of cosmic acceleration
mapping.b_element: the cosmological constant Lambda taken as a universal constant measuring residual spacetime curvature

brief_justification: The only overlap is the shared object 'cosmological constant Lambda', not shared machinery. Paper A is observational statistical inference of the expansion history from distance measurements (SN Ia luminosity distances, BAO/CMB angular scales, growth of density fluctuations), whereas Paper B is a group-theoretic reformulation of special-relativity kinematics based on the de Sitter group SO(1,4); there is no common governing equation, dynamical law, or estimation principle linking measurement-based cosmography to a symmetry-postulate kinematics, so no role-for-role mapping holds.
